comment_text.sn12.31:1.3: Ajita was one of a group of contemplative brahmins from a school that identified the cosmic divinity with the state of purified formless consciousness experienced in deep meditation. Their story is recounted in “The Way to the Far Shore”, where Ajita was the first to question the Buddha (Snp 5.2). The title is given in the text itself (Snp 5.18:8.4, Snp 5.19:1.1), and it is cited also in AN 3.32, AN 3.33, AN 4.41, AN 7.53, and AN 6.61.
comment_text.sn12.31:3.1: In reply to Ajita, the Buddha already answered the question: a mendicant without greed, skillful, would wander mindful. Here he asks for a detailed analysis, presumably as a teaching method: the dialogue was probably in front of an assembly.
comment_text.sn12.31:3.2: Sāriputta hesitates, according to the commentary, because he is unsure of the mode of analysis that the Buddha wants him to pursue. This is typical of Sāriputta’s circumspection; he himself explains a similar hesitation in the next Sutta (SN 12.32:16.2).
comment_text.sn12.31:7.1: See MN 38:9.1, where “this has come to be” refers to dependently originated consciousness. There, the Buddha is refuting Sāti, who believed consciousness is “the one who speaks, the one who knows, who experiences the results of good and bad deeds in all the different realms.” This is the Upaniṣadic view, which was apparently shared by both Ajita and Sāti, and against which the Buddha posited dependent origination.
comment_text.sn12.31:7.4: “Originated with that as fuel” is also at MN 38:9.3, cementing the connection between these two texts.
comment_text.sn12.31:7.8: Note that Sāriputta does not answer Ajita’s question on practice; rather, he analyzes the terms. This illustrates how analytical commentary developed from practical advice.
comment_text.sn12.31:8.3: The stream-enterer sees the truth, the arahant is fully freed.

root_text.sn12.31:1.2: Tatra kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn12.31:3.1: Imassa nu kho, sāriputta, saṅkhittena bhāsitassa kathaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.sn12.31:3.2: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn12.31:4.1: Dutiyampi kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ āmantesi …pe…
root_text.sn12.31:4.2: dutiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn12.31:4.3: Tatiyampi kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn12.31:6.1: Imassa nu kho, sāriputta, saṅkhittena bhāsitassa kathaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.sn12.31:6.2: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn12.31:7.3: Bhūtamidanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā bhūtassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:7.5: Tadāhārasambhavanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā āhārasambhavassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:7.6: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.sn12.31:7.7: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā nirodhadhammassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:8.3: Bhūtamidanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā bhūtassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:8.5: Tadāhārasambhavanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā āhārasambhavassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:8.6: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.sn12.31:8.7: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā nirodhadhammassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:8.9: Iti kho, bhante, yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ pārāyane ajitapañhe:
root_text.sn12.31:10.1: Imassa khvāhaṁ, bhante, saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn12.31:11.1: “Sādhu sādhu, sāriputta, bhūtamidanti, sāriputta, yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.sn12.31:11.2: Bhūtamidanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā bhūtassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:11.4: Tadāhārasambhavanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā āhārasambhavassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:11.5: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.sn12.31:11.6: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā nirodhadhammassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:12.3: Bhūtamidanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā bhūtassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:12.5: Tadāhārasambhavanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā āhārasambhavassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:12.6: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.sn12.31:12.7: Tadāhāranirodhā yaṁ bhūtaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññā disvā nirodhadhammassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto hoti.
root_text.sn12.31:12.9: Iti kho, sāriputta, yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ pārāyane ajitapañhe:
root_text.sn12.31:14.1: Imassa kho, sāriputta, saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn12.31:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn12.31:1.2: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Sāriputta,
translation_text.sn12.31:1.3: “Sāriputta, this was said in ‘The Way to the Far Shore’, in ‘The Questions of Ajita’:
translation_text.sn12.31:2.1: ‘There are those who have appraised the teaching,
translation_text.sn12.31:2.2: and many kinds of trainees here.
translation_text.sn12.31:2.3: Now you, being alert, are asked
translation_text.sn12.31:2.4: to tell me their behavior, good fellow.’
translation_text.sn12.31:3.1: How should we see the detailed meaning of this brief statement?”
translation_text.sn12.31:3.2: When he said this, Sāriputta kept silent.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.1: “Sāriputta, do you see that this has come to be?”
translation_text.sn12.31:7.2: “Sir, one truly sees with right wisdom that this has come to be.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.3: Seeing this, one is practicing for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding what has come to be.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.4: One truly sees with right wisdom that it originated with that as fuel.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.5: Seeing this, one is practicing for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding the fuel for its origination.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.6: One truly sees with right wisdom that when that fuel ceases, what has come to be is liable to cease.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.7: Seeing this, one is practicing for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding what is liable to cease.
translation_text.sn12.31:7.8: In this way one is a trainee.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.1: And what, sir, is one who has appraised the teaching?
translation_text.sn12.31:8.2: Sir, one truly sees with right wisdom that this has come to be.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.3: Seeing this, one is freed by not grasping through disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding what has come to be.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.4: One truly sees with right wisdom that it originated with that as fuel.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.5: Seeing this, one is freed by not grasping through disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding the fuel for its origination.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.6: One truly sees with right wisdom that when that fuel ceases, what has come to be is liable to cease.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.7: Seeing this, one is freed by not grasping through disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding what is liable to cease.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.8: In this way one has appraised the teaching.
translation_text.sn12.31:8.9: Sir, regarding what was said in ‘The Way to the Far Shore’, in ‘The Questions of Ajita’:
translation_text.sn12.31:9.1: ‘There are those who have appraised the teaching,
translation_text.sn12.31:9.2: and many kinds of trainees here.
translation_text.sn12.31:9.3: Now you, being alert, are asked
translation_text.sn12.31:9.4: to tell me their behavior, good fellow.’
translation_text.sn12.31:10.1: This is how I understand the detailed meaning of what was said in brief.”
translation_text.sn12.31:11.1: “Good, good, Sāriputta!” (The Buddha repeated all of Sāriputta’s explanation, concluding:)
translation_text.sn12.31:14.1: This is how to understand the detailed meaning of what was said in brief.”
